item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Holy crap that was disturbing. And I've read, I dunno, close to a thousand skips at this point. +1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Downvote. I do like this SCP, but the containment procedures are lacking. It doesn't address what happens if an instance of SCP 1003-2 is found out in the wild - Like, say one of these kids does a DNA test and it doesn't turn out the way it's supposed to, and that then catches the attention of a foundation agent. What are they supposed to do in this instance?

item.title: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded: Noting that a recent revision was reverted, as it was an unnecessary change that introduced an error. "Faeces" is an alternate spelling of "feces", and is not to be replaced with "faces".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That photo scared the daylights out of me. Other than that, It's a great SCP.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Truly terrifying, but it is pretty similar to 1486. Still a good idea and read nonetheless.
+1

item.title: eek
item.content:encoded: Its a cool SCP and is strange like alot or my favorite SCPs are, but that picure is so creepy. Imagine waking up and turning your head to that in your bed with you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: troll post, ignore - ARD

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Tapeworm Child? That sounds interestOABHJGKrlwy85359v

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Rather than the generic horror of parasite babies, this skip makes me curious of the lives of the children who forget who they were and if they could live in society as an anomaly. Seeing your loved one get infected by you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Picture ruins it for me too. It just seems to me that if I had something like that in my body, I would notice. I also don't like the development stages of the SCP, as they seem to be there soley to justify use of the picture.

That being said, I think the idea is absolutely terrifying so I won't downvote, but I think this had a lot more potential.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Solid downvote for me.

It's pretty basic as far as parasitic skips go1, and the 'species masquerading as human' creep factor fell flat, as there's plenty of other skips2 that tackle that concept in a stronger fashion.

TBH, I feel like the image played a hand in pulling in upvotes, this simply doesn't feel like a strong submission.

Footnotes
1. "SCP-400 Beautiful Babies" destroys this on that front
2. "The Adults", "The Harrison-Grey Effect" for starters

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I clicked on this article thinking "oh, this looks like a reference to the System Of A Down song 'Needles'. What I got was completely unexpected and caused me to jump from my seat, yell, and drop my phone. Upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like this SCP, but I don't understand why it's keter? It's not like SCP-217, where it can be contracted be just contact with an infected individual, not to mention, SCP-217 is not treatable at all, and this SCP doesn't state it's not treatable. The containment procedures, while specific, is not really worthy of a keter, but more of a euclid class. this should be downgraded to euclid, but it still gets a +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: May I suggest replacing parasite with parasitoid? A typical parasite lives off its host, not killing it when it has matured. Parasitoids kill their host when they have developed enough.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Aside from the "OH GOD NECROMORPH BABY" jumpscare when the page loaded, this is still cool. Bloody [EXPLETIVE REDACTED] chestbursters-turned-humans with contagious properties…

And now I wonder what would happen if they ever ran out of humans to infect in the planet, or whether a fully-grown 1003-2 would have an existential crisis upon learning of how it's what it is instead of a human.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I figured it's because of how extremely contagious SCP-1003-2 are.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
OH SHIT WHY THAT PICTURE…..

You'd think I'd learn not to click on random SCP's before checking the Nightmare Fuel section on TVTropes…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The tone could use some tightening up, and there isn't much of a story here, but otherwise this isn't bad (that picture, though, is another matter entirely).

Parasite skips rarely do it for me, mainly because they usually don't feel that anomalous, but that's certainly not a problem here. A rewrite of this, with more emphasis on storytelling and body horror, could be great.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Necroposting again with basically the same message.

No, they won't. The development stage they excrete is not the one that infects humans. They're going to infect dogs etc, which in turn can infect people with the scary form.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Necroposting a bit, but whatever.

Tapeworms have multiple hosts, which suitable for a different development stage. The form this SCP spreads can infect carnivores, hooved animals are unsuitable for them to live in.

That also makes the scenario "one adult tapeman touches a doorknob, thousands die eaten by babies" impossible". That reduces the creep factor a bit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah I've seen that image way too many times elsewhere to upvote this. Plus it's just a generic "spooky" picture. I wouldn't upvote an article using smiledog any more than this one.

It ruins the article for me. Novote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: [EXPLETIVE REDACTED] this is disgusting/terrifying. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm assuming they haven't been let go.

They haven't all been caught yet.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, this is precisely why I downvoted.
I can't believe no-one's addressed this yet.

(Sorry if this is constitutes a necropost)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The picture loads really fast on my screen and makes me jump every time. Over all it's good

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't think this has been addressed before but

Instances of SCP-1003-2 are usually taken into orphanages and sometimes adopted by foster parents.

So does this mean that the Foundation releases SCP-1003-2 out into the world? Or just that instances found outside Foundation control are taken to orphanages by people ignorant to their true nature? Because considering the anomalous properties they still possess, and the threat of an outbreak that they pose (parasite passed on through saliva? Are we just assuming these new humans are never gonna kiss someone else?), I really doubt the Foundation would just let them go like that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
And then they become normal human beings and grow up with no knowledge of ever being a cannibalistic tapeworm - except that their sweat and saliva contain tapeworm protoscolex, which infect carnivores and develop into SCP-1003-1, thus continuing the cycle!

Everyone they ever kiss will die because of them. ()_()

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "neonate" means newborn.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-1003-2's development mostly follows the pattern of normal human prenatal development, but at a faster rate. By 8 weeks, it is as mature as a 3-week-old neonate, although it is similar in size to an 8-week-old embryo (approximately 13-16 cm).

I'm confused by this. Is it only as developed as a three week old, but the size of an eight week old? That doesn't seem accelerated. Is it supposed to be 3-month-old?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh god I opened the page and the picture… that picture scared the living shit out of me. Nobody had better pull this crap again. Seriously. Now, I'm going to go change my pants and actually read the article. *waddles off*

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't like it. The tone is hell weird.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah…The doll in the photo used to have a visible seam at the shoulder, made it look totally fake. I complained about it at the time, but in retrospect, I might have preferred it stay fake-looking >.<

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Whoever put that picture there.

You're a jerk. Now I'm not gonna get any sleep.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I dont see how this isnt keter.
Its pretty much impossible to contain and one infected can do a lot of damage.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good job on spotting that. I'll go clear it up right now…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it, but I feel that it should not be Keter unless it is very hard to control. it seems like there is a low infection rate according to the article. also, shouldn't it say 13-16 cm not 5-6 inch?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My first reaction on seeing the picture on entering the page while tired was holy Jesus!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's something of an unpleasant surprise to me how much more unsettling the whole thing became once I didn't have the visible shoulder seam to reassure myself that it was obviously fake…

Upvote give. Page never-look-at-again. Eeeeee…

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for the advice. I've just sorted out those little things you mentioned.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really do like this SCP, I just think that some of the language used is a bit too informal for the SCP Foundation. For example;

…may only do so for the express purpose of research, must be Level 4, and must have written permission from…

I recommend changing the underlined text to something different, like:

…may only do so for the express purpose of research, must have Level 4 security clearance, and must have written permission from…

That seems a lot more formal. In the first example, it mentioned Level 4, but Level 4 what? Level 4 of that motel down the street? You need to mention security when mentioning security levels. Remember, a handy tip is to always write as if it will be the first SCP someone will read.

Also, try to avoid using ███ in the containment. It's supposed to be a file so that people know how to contain it, if we don't know what that info is, how do we know what to or what not to do?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Shopped out the joint seams, but didn't want to change the picture without the author's permission.

Picture under cut
Ahhh get rid of it

Author permission get. Changed pic.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Before even reading the article, I just have to say that the ad at the bottom of the screen is asking me to adopt a baby.

I'm not sure that there is a worse possible place for that ad to be than on a page with a picture like that…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like this, but one thing has been bugging me (pun absolutely intended).
When you say animals of the order Carnivora aren't allowed near 1003-2, I assume this is because they take carnivores as hosts. But Carnivora only covers mammals. I would recomend saying that carnivorous animals aren't to be allowed near it, but given what you said about the sheep, I would say not to let any animal near 1003-2.
It's just a little thing though, and i will still upvote it because the idea is still fantastic.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think that picture will be burned into my mind for the rest of my life. Honestly, I thought that was some deformed baby… *squick* Every time I try to read the article, my eyes drift back to that image.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: EDIT: My apologies, not the first to notice that.

item.title: SCP?
item.content:encoded:
I'm a noob so forgive me for my ignorance. What exactly is the SCP?
adults

larvae

hosts

So I'm just confused exactly what SCP-1003 is. Is it the whole process of all of this going down or is it a physical something?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sorry, I wasn't aware of the alternate spelling used in Britain. I returned it to it's original spelling.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This article always makes me squirm when I read it. A great piece of work, got my upvote easily.

Also, I fixed a typo.

where it produces eggs that are passed in the host's faeces.

Changed it to:

where it produces eggs that are passed in the host's feces.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I remember seeing this one some time ago. Exquisitely disgusting, but the picture is fucking awful. No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Months later, yoric reopened this page and realized the pic makes the article awful and downvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The article itself is servicable aside from the "It magically knows stuff it couldn't" part, but that picture wrecks it. Biological impossibility aside, the visible shoulder joint makes it very hard to see it as anything other than a doll.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I doubt you'd consider it, but I think the picture would be a lot creepier if you censored the eyes. Just put a big black bar across.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ugh. Creepy. I'd think that "humanoid" forms of 1003 would act in such a way as to spread its kind without realizing it is doing so. Eating, kissing, sexual intercourse…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why would the Foundation keep 10 of these creatures? They are not dependent on each other. I figure they would destroy all but a couple to make containment easier.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes, I want to see more SCPs like this. I can totally see a tale about an agent discovering he is one of these things.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like this concept, particularly the fact that once they're 'born' they just look and act like regular babies but sweat the parasite oh god.

That said, I recognize the line of ddolls the image is from, so it was very very hard for me to take that picture seriously.

This. All of this. It wasn't enough to make me not upvote, but I would strongly suggest replacing that image or just getting rid of it altogether.

If I pretend the image isn't there, this article is pretty fantastic. Lots of great detail.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
To me, it seems like an unnecessarily advanced organism that emulates humans perfectly.

Even if this was a correct statement (and it isn't; see Khym Chanur's post) that would still be highly anomalous. Just how the fuck would something like that evolve? Yes, if it did manage to, it would probably be pretty successful, but there's no way it could ever possibly get there. Which leaves genetic engineering as the only non-anomalous explanation, and we don't have that kind of technology and won't for at least quite some time, which makes the technology and thus the product of the technology anomalous.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1. It has the DNA of an ordinary tapeworm, but grows to adulthood in a massively different manner than an ordinary tapeworm.
  2. After being "born", it can have skills and knowledge that aren't encoded in DNA.

Those are two things that should be impossible according to science as we know it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I am kind of missing the anomalous part of this. To me, it seems like an unnecessarily advanced organism that emulates humans perfectly. It is very creepy, but all I see when I read this article is a larvae that grows up to become a person. It also seems like a job more fitting of CDC than SCP.

No vote for now.
